value.0: Hold, prisoner. The <CUSTOM100> you carry bears unlawful enchantment — more power bound into one piece than any honest forge of Middle-earth may grant. <CUSTOM6119> You will go no further until it is stripped back within the law. Submit it to me, and I will unmake what was wrongly made. There is no refund for stolen power.
Your gear is clean, traveler. You are free to go — and see that you keep to honest forges from now on.
Which enchantment shall I strike from the <CUSTOM100>? <CUSTOM6119> The magic is lost forever, and the law pays nothing for unmaking.

The law of the forges is plain: no modified item may carry more than six enchantments, nor be worked past seven hundred and fifty thousand gold in worth. Relics of the old world that were born mighty are no crime — but gear hammered past those bounds at a forge is contraband, and its bearer sits here until it is made lawful again.
Strike the <CUSTOM6118> from it? There is no refund and no undoing.
It is done. The <CUSTOM100> stands lawful once more, and I will unmake no more of it. <CUSTOM6119>
So be it. The <CUSTOM100> is remade as the smiths first forged it — every added enchantment unmade, and not a copper returned. <CUSTOM6119>
This piece defies remaking — its first forging is written in no ledger I hold. Its enchantments we must unmake one by one, by hand.
It is done. Every unlawful piece you carried is remade as it was first forged, and not a copper returned. All your gear stands lawful — now begone, and keep to honest forges.
Most of it is remade — but some pieces defy remaking, their first forging written in no ledger I hold. Those we must unmake by hand before you walk free.

Then the law will weigh your word. The <CUSTOM100> is sequestered under seal in the House of Homer, where the stewards of the Valar will judge its provenance. If your claim holds true, it shall be returned to you; if not, it is forfeit. Either way, it burdens you no longer.
Strip an enchantment from it.
Why is this gear illegal?
I will sit here a while longer.
<CUSTOM6110>
<CUSTOM6111>
<CUSTOM6112>
<CUSTOM6113>
<CUSTOM6114>
<CUSTOM6115>
<CUSTOM6116>
<CUSTOM6117>
Never mind.
Yes, strike it. The magic is forfeit.
No, leave it be.
Release me. My gear is lawful.
Very well. Let us begin the unmaking.
Good day, Warden.
What of the rest of my gear?
That is all. Good day, Warden.
Remake the <CUSTOM100> as it was first forged. (lose ALL added enchantments)
Remake ALL my unlawful gear as first forged, and release me now.
This <CUSTOM100> came to me honestly - bought or won fair in Middle-earth, not forged by my hand. I dispute the charge. (item held for DM review)
